Title: The OXI1 Kinase Pathway Mediates Piriformospora indica-Induced Growth Promotion in Arabidopsis

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Class: lipid signaling metabolite; phospholipid-derived second messenger
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Second messenger associated with plant growth regulation and stress signaling
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for Piriformospora indica-induced growth promotion downstream of PA/PDK1 signaling; does not mediate defense-gene activation under beneficial conditions
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in roots and shoots; induced in roots by H2O2 in prior work
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: ~4-fold upregulated in P. indica-colonized roots; absent in oxi1 mutant; reduced in pii12 promoter mutant roots and shoots
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native promoter affected in pii12 by 19-bp upstream deletion; complementation with full-length OXI1 cDNA
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: PDK1.1; PDK1.2; AGC2-2 (pathway-associated homolog); MPK6 (proposed downstream target from prior work)
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: oxi1 mutants have reduced root hair length and lose fungal growth-promotion response
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Identified by EMS screen as pii12 with promoter deletion; overexpression behaved like wild type, suggesting endogenous OXI1 level is sufficient; fungal colonization was not impaired in oxi1
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. EMS mutant pii12 lacking normal OXI1 expression failed to show P. indica-induced growth promotion. 2. Complementation with OXI1 restored the response, and an independent oxi1 T-DNA line confirmed the requirement. 3. OXI1 transcript accumulated in colonized roots, but defense-gene repression/activation patterns were independent of the OXI1 pathway under the tested conditions.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: EMS mutant mapping; promoter-sequence analysis; complementation with full-length cDNA; T-DNA knockout analysis; qRT-PCR; growth phenotyping; colonization assay
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Beneficial co-cultivation with Piriformospora indica; comparison with high-dose non-beneficial fungal lawn
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to improve beneficial microbe-responsive growth signaling, but manipulation should consider developmental roles and lack of gain-of-function benefit from overexpression

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Class: lipid signaling metabolite; phospholipid-derived second messenger
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Second messenger associated with plant growth regulation and stress signaling
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for P. indica-induced growth promotion, likely acting downstream of PA/PDK1 signaling
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: AGC serine/threonine protein kinase; OXI1 homolog with conserved PDK1-binding FxxF motif
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in Arabidopsis; not ROS-inducible according to prior knowledge cited
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Single mutant loses growth-promotion response; inability to recover homozygous oxi1 agc2-2 double mutants suggests essential developmental role
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Root hairs and colonization were normal in agc2-2; defense-gene regulation during beneficial and non-beneficial interactions was independent of AGC2-2
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. agc2-2 mutants were impaired in fungal growth promotion despite normal colonization. 2. AGC2-2 is the closest OXI1 homolog and contains a conserved PDK1-binding motif. 3. Failure to obtain homozygous oxi1 agc2-2 double mutants suggests additional developmental importance.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: T-DNA knockout analysis; growth phenotyping; qRT-PCR; colonization assay; genetic crossing
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Beneficial co-cultivation with Piriformospora indica; high-dose fungal treatment for defense comparison
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential component of beneficial symbiosis signaling, but developmental essentiality cautions against simple knockout-based engineering

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Class: lipid signaling metabolite; phospholipid-derived second messenger
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Second messenger involved in growth regulation, biomass production, cell expansion, and biotic/abiotic stress responses
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for P. indica-mediated growth promotion because reduced PA synthesis compromises the response
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Structural phospholipids (e.g., phosphatidylcholine; specific substrate not tested in this paper)
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyzes membrane phospholipids to generate phosphatidic acid as a lipid second messenger
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step for PA production in roots supported by mutant phenotype and prior evidence cited
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Not induced by P. indica according to Discussion; constitutively abundant in roots
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: pldα1 mutants are impaired in fungal growth-promotion response; prior studies cited indicate reduced root PA levels
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: P. indica exudates stimulated PA accumulation; PLDα1 was inferred as a major source because pldα1 mutants showed impaired response whereas pldα3 and pldε did not
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. P. indica exudate induced PA accumulation in roots. 2. pldα1 mutants showed strongly reduced growth promotion by the fungus. 3. The data support PLDα1-mediated PA production as an upstream component of the PLD-PDK1-OXI1 signaling cascade.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: T-DNA knockout analysis; growth phenotyping; 32Pi-labeling and TLC-based PA quantification after fungal exudate treatment
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Treatment with P. indica exudates; beneficial co-cultivation with P. indica
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to enhance beneficial fungus-induced growth via lipid signaling, but manipulation may broadly affect stress and membrane-signaling processes

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Class: lipid signaling metabolite; phospholipid-derived second messenger
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Second messenger involved in growth regulation and stress signaling
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for full P. indica-mediated growth promotion, consistent with a role in PA synthesis upstream of PDK1/OXI1
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Structural phospholipids (specific substrate not tested in this paper)
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyzes membrane phospholipids to produce phosphatidic acid
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key contributing control step for fungus-responsive PA production inferred from mutant phenotype
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Not induced by P. indica according to Discussion; constitutively abundant in roots
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: pldδ mutants are impaired in fungal growth-promotion response
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Unlike PLDα3 and PLDε, PLDδ was specifically required for the beneficial growth response; H2O2-linked activation known from prior literature was not supported under this beneficial interaction
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. pldδ mutants were severely impaired in P. indica-induced growth promotion. 2. The evidence places PLDδ with PLDα1 upstream of PA accumulation and PDK1 activation. 3. Its role in this system appears independent of H2O2 accumulation, which was not induced by the fungus.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: T-DNA knockout analysis; growth phenotyping; inference from PA induction by fungal exudates and pathway genetics
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Beneficial co-cultivation with P. indica; fungal exudate signaling
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for engineering beneficial microbe responsiveness, but broad signaling roles may create pleiotropic effects

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Class: lipid signaling metabolite; phospholipid-derived second messenger
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Second messenger-linked control of growth and signaling
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Together with PDK1.2, required for P. indica-induced growth promotion downstream of PA and upstream of OXI1/AGC2-2
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential upstream kinase in the PA-PDK1-OXI1 cascade mediating fungus-induced growth promotion
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates downstream AGC kinases OXI1 and likely AGC2-2
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Kinase activation downstream of lipid second messenger PA
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in roots; ~2-fold upregulated in colonized roots
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: pdk1.1 pdk1.2 double mutants are smaller, have shorter siliques, and produce only ~41% of wild-type seed set
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Beneficial co-cultivation with P. indica; high-dose fungal lawn comparison
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PDK1.1 and PDK1.2 transcripts increased in colonized roots. 2. Double knockout plants lacked fungal growth-promotion despite normal colonization. 3. The data place PDK1 upstream of OXI1/AGC2-2 in a PA-responsive cascade.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Single-gene contribution was not resolved genetically in isolation; paper emphasized the combined requirement of PDK1.1 and PDK1.2
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Generation and RT-PCR confirmation of pdk1.1 pdk1.2 double knockout; growth phenotyping; qRT-PCR; colonization assay
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A potentially powerful leverage point for beneficial symbiosis signaling, but strong developmental penalties in double-loss lines indicate careful tuning rather than knockout
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: phosphatidic acid (PA) induced by P. indica exudates

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Class: lipid signaling metabolite; phospholipid-derived second messenger
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Second messenger-linked control of growth and signaling
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Together with PDK1.1, required for P. indica-induced growth promotion downstream of PA and upstream of OXI1/AGC2-2
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential upstream kinase in the PA-PDK1-OXI1 cascade mediating fungus-induced growth promotion
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates downstream AGC kinases OXI1 and likely AGC2-2
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Kinase activation downstream of lipid second messenger PA
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in roots; ~2-fold upregulated in colonized roots
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Contributes to severe developmental defects in pdk1.1 pdk1.2 double mutants
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Beneficial co-cultivation with P. indica; high-dose fungal lawn comparison
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PDK1.2 expression increased in colonized roots. 2. Combined loss with PDK1.1 abolished fungal growth promotion without affecting colonization. 3. The evidence supports PDK1.2 as part of a PA-responsive kinase relay upstream of OXI1 and AGC2-2.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Functional inference was made primarily through the pdk1.1 pdk1.2 double mutant because redundancy is high
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Generation and RT-PCR confirmation of pdk1.1 pdk1.2 double knockout; growth phenotyping; qRT-PCR; colonization assay
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful signaling node for engineering microbe-induced growth, but redundancy and developmental side effects require fine control
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: phosphatidic acid (PA) induced by P. indica exudates
